name: Engineering Standards
description: NZ infrastructure design standards (IDS), earthworks and compaction requirements (NZS 4431), geotechnical QA procedures, and subdivision consent documentation for practitioners working in the Canterbury / NZ regulatory context.
use_cases: Checking NZ engineering design standards for geotechnical work
Verifying earthworks and compaction requirements
Understanding subdivision consent documentation requirements

name: Ground Engineering Magazine
description: Issues of Ground Engineering magazine (2014-2026) covering geotechnical industry news, technical papers, and key standards development (Eurocode 7, BS 5930, SPERWall, CIRIA) across piling, foundations, tunnelling, earthworks, and slope stability.
use_cases: Researching geotechnical engineering standards development
Finding technical papers on piling, foundations, or slope stability
Understanding industry trends in geotechnical engineering

name: Geotechnical Baseline Reports (GBR)
description: Industry guidance on preparing and contractually applying GBRs to allocate subsurface construction risk. Covers Differing Site Conditions claims, ASCE guidelines, CIRIA C807, the NZTS guide, and the FIDIC Emerald Book.
use_cases: Preparing geotechnical baseline reports
Understanding subsurface risk allocation in contracts
Researching differing site conditions claims

name: Geotechnical Engineering Report Workflows and Standard Procedures
description: Professional practice guides, templates, QA checklists, and training materials for geotechnical report structuring, factual/interpretive delineation, and client-centric writing -- compiled from a leading NZ geotechnical consultancy's internal knowledge base.
use_cases: Structuring geotechnical engineering reports
Applying factual/interpretive delineation in reports
Using QA checklists for report review

name: Risk Assessment in Engineering
description: Risk management, professional liability, and probabilistic risk assessment in geotechnical engineering. Sources span ASCE, CIRIA, and NZGS guidelines, field manuals, and legal notes covering contracts, non-disclosure agreements, and professional indemnity insurance.
use_cases: Assessing geotechnical engineering risk
Understanding professional liability in engineering
Reviewing contract and insurance requirements

name: AI System Engineering
description: Generative AI application design and deployment: foundation models, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), multiagent orchestration, and FastAPI/Docker serving. Covers MCP, OAuth2, the NIST AI Risk Management Framework, the EU AI Act, and GDPR.
use_cases: Designing generative AI applications
Implementing RAG or multiagent systems
Understanding AI governance and compliance requirements

name: Software Architecture & Domain-Driven Design
description: Ten canonical books (Evans, Vernon, Khononov, Ford, Ousterhout, GoF, and others) covering DDD strategic/tactical patterns (Bounded Contexts, Ubiquitous Language, Aggregates, Repositories, Domain Events), architecture styles (Hexagonal, CQRS, Microservices, Event-Driven), SOLID principles, GoF design patterns, functional domain modeling, EventStorming, Context Mapping, Event Sourcing, saga patterns, and Data Mesh.
use_cases: Applying DDD patterns to Redline architecture
Choosing architecture styles for new modules
Reviewing design patterns for domain modeling

name: Business Process Management
description: BPM methodologies and continuous process improvement tools including Lean, Six Sigma, BPMN, 7FE, PDCA, and DMAIC frameworks. Drawn from expert-authored management books and the BPM Common Body of Knowledge (BPM CBOK).
use_cases: Designing or improving business processes
Applying Lean or Six Sigma to engineering workflows
Modelling processes with BPMN

name: Product Roadmapping
description: Strategic product roadmapping for product leaders: defining vision, prioritising customer opportunities, and achieving stakeholder buy-in. Covers Kano model, MoSCoW prioritisation, ROI scorecards, user journey maps, and opportunity-solution trees.
use_cases: Building or refreshing the product roadmap
Prioritising features using Kano or MoSCoW
Creating opportunity-solution trees

name: Writing Painless Product and Functional Specifications
description: Software product management frameworks and specification writing: PRDs, functional specifications, OKRs, the Shape Up framework, product discovery vs delivery, and alternatives to traditional roadmaps.
use_cases: Writing product requirement documents
Structuring functional specifications
Choosing between product discovery and delivery approaches

name: Engineers' Guide to Technical Communication
description: Technical writing and editing best practices for engineering and scientific audiences: document design, audience analysis, ethics, citation standards (Chicago Manual of Style), and structure for reports, proposals, laboratory studies, specifications, and business correspondence.
use_cases: Structuring engineering reports and proposals
Applying citation and documentation standards
Improving technical writing quality

name: Information Architecture and Knowledge Management
description: Explores the strategic design and management of information systems to make organizational knowledge assets findable, understandable, and actionable. Covers foundational concepts of Information Architecture, Knowledge Architectures, and Knowledge Management.
use_cases: Designing information architectures for knowledge systems
Improving findability and knowledge organisation
Structuring knowledge management strategies

name: Redline Founder Memos
description: Transcribed voice memos recorded by the Redline founder (April 2026) covering product vision, feature design, go-to-market strategy, pricing tiers, competitive analysis (vs. ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini), UX decisions (the 'Burton' email-based interface), and personal reflections on building a niche AI tool for small geotechnical consultancies. Primary source for founder intent and strategic rationale.
use_cases: Understanding founder intent and strategic rationale
Grounding strategy work in original product vision
Informing GTM and pricing decisions

name: Monetizing & Scaling Innovation
description: Strategic guide on building profitable, scalable products. Contains Monetizing Innovation and Scaling Innovation by Madhavan Ramanujam et al. Covers willingness-to-pay discovery, pricing models (subscription, usage-based, outcome-based), packaging and bundling (Good-Better-Best tiers), behavioural pricing tactics (anchoring, compromise effect), the four monetisation failure patterns (Feature Shock, Minivation, Hidden Gem, Undead), and scaling strategies covering churn prevention, wallet-share growth, and price negotiation discipline.
use_cases: Deciding how to price a new product, feature tier, or service
Evaluating packaging and Good-Better-Best tier strategies
Preparing for a company-wide price increase or sales negotiation
Designing a product go-to-market strategy grounded in customer willingness-to-pay
Researching known monetisation failure patterns before a product launch

name: Entrepreneurship & Startup Strategy
description: Comprehensive guide for entrepreneurial management, strategic growth, and corporate innovation. Covers disruptive innovation, Lean Startup methodology (MVP, Build-Measure-Learn), customer development and Jobs-to-Be-Done theory, product-market fit, Crossing the Chasm (early adopters vs mainstream), B2B sales tactics, product roadmapping, and the Business Model Canvas. Drawn from authoritative business books, case studies, and operational frameworks.
use_cases: Launching a new venture and identifying a scalable business model
Bringing a disruptive technology to market without falling into traditional management traps
Restructuring a mature organisation to foster an entrepreneurial culture
Designing a B2B sales strategy targeting the right corporate decision-makers
Creating a product roadmap that aligns teams around customer value

name: Digital Marketing & Social Selling
description: Comprehensive educational guide for modern digital marketing, online sales, and personal branding. Covers content marketing (They Ask, You Answer / The Big 5), SEO and Product-Led SEO, LinkedIn social selling, Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) targeting, and using Generative AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini) for content and SEO research. Drawn from eight specialised books and professional guides on marketing, sales, and technology.
use_cases: Increasing website traffic and conversion rates through content marketing
Building a personal brand and professional network on LinkedIn
Executing SEO strategy using Product-Led or traditional keyword approaches
Aligning sales and marketing teams around shared goals
Using AI tools to assist with content and SEO research

name: Product Design & UX
description: Four canonical texts on human-centred design and usability: The Design of Everyday Things (Norman), Don't Make Me Think Revisited (Krug), Designing with Data, and Forms that Work. Covers affordances, signifiers, the psychology of human error, A/B testing, usability testing, web navigation, and form design.
use_cases: Designing intuitive web interfaces or digital products using HCD principles
Setting up usability tests or A/B experiments to resolve design debates
Optimising form design for user comprehension and completion rates
Diagnosing why users make errors or abandon a system
Grounding product UX decisions in psychology and empirical evidence

name: Organisational Design & Team Topologies
description: A curated collection of books on modern organisational leadership, team design, and workforce development. Covers Team Topologies (stream-aligned, platform, enabling teams), Conway's Law, cognitive load management, AI/RPA task deconstruction, deliberate practice, skills-based organisations, and Domain-Driven Design (DDD) for software teams. Case studies from Google, GE, and large healthcare providers.
topics: AI and automation in the workplace
use_cases: Restructuring software engineering teams around stream-aligned or platform topology
Deciding which tasks to automate with AI or RPA versus retain for human workers
Designing deliberate practice programs to develop world-class technical talent
Transitioning from role-based to skills-based hiring and performance management
Applying Conway's Law and DDD to align team structure with software architecture

name: Professional Services Firm Management
description: Comprehensive guide to the business, management, and future of professional service firms (PSFs) — engineering, law, and consulting practices. Covers individual career development (resume writing, networking, PE licensure), firm creation and entrepreneurship, day-to-day operations (project management, client relations, IDC), financial and human capital management, A/E/C firm strategy, and technological disruption (the Innovator's Dilemma, AI impact on professions). Contains practical templates, checklists, and worksheets alongside theoretical frameworks from published books and management literature.
use_cases: Managing or growing a professional services or engineering firm
Planning a career in engineering or consulting
Assessing risks from technological disruption to professional service business models
Improving firm operations — project management, client relations, and profitability
Researching A/E/C firm strategy, niche positioning, and market differentiation

name: Government Contracting, Proposal Management & Systems Engineering
description: Comprehensive guide to winning government contracts (GovCon). Covers requirements engineering (RE), Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) using UML and SysML, proposal strategy and management, RFP compliance and compliance matrices, and AI-assisted proposal automation including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for hallucination-free draft generation. Contains academic textbooks, practical playbooks, compliance matrix templates, decision matrices, and checklists.
use_cases: Preparing a winning bid for a government contract
Building requirements engineering frameworks for complex system design
Adopting AI to automate proposal compliance and draft generation
Diagnosing and fixing broken proposal processes
Translating vague customer requirements into precise, testable system specifications

name: Legal AI Startup
description: Y Combinator interview with Max Junestrand, CEO of Leya (leya.law, $675M valuation, YC W24; original source transcribed name as 'Legora' — corrected 2026-04-27), plus Leya website content. Covers legal AI product strategy, playbook-driven contract review, Word-native integration, senior-sponsor GTM in professional services, audit trails, and the collapse of point solutions under generative AI.
use_cases: Analysing adjacent-market signals for document-heavy AI products
Understanding playbook-driven review as a product pattern
Comparing legal AI adoption dynamics to engineering AI adoption
Extracting GTM lessons for selling AI into professional services firms
Studying the workspace-over-chatbot product framing

name: Geotechnical Engineering Checklists
description: A curated collection of geotechnical engineering QC checklists, review guidelines, and submittal checklists from US and NZ sources. Covers geotechnical report review (FHWA), geotechnical design QC workflow (work plan, soils/geology report, foundation reports, final plans), earthquake geotechnical investigation practice (NZGS Module 2), ground investigation specification (NZGS_0200), and building consent lodgement requirements for geotechnical submissions.
use_cases: Reviewing a geotechnical report for completeness and quality
Checking required content for a soils and geology report or foundation report
Understanding FHWA or USACE requirements for geotechnical documentation
Verifying a geotechnical report meets NZ building consent lodgement requirements
Applying NZGS ground investigation specifications to site investigation planning
